senses.definition: the process or activity of exchanging information or expressing feelings, thoughts, or ideas by speaking, writing, etc.

examples.x: Good communication is key to a successful relationship.
Her excellent communication skills make her a great team leader.
Learning a new language can enhance your communication with people from different cultures.
The company believes in open and honest communication among its employees.
Writing letters was a common form of communication in the past.

senses.definition: a talk that is between two or more people and they tell each other about different things like feelings, ideas, and thoughts

examples.x: During our conversation, I learned he had lived in Italy for five years.
I overheard an interesting conversation at the coffee shop.
Our teacher encouraged us to have a conversation in English.
The conversation drifted from politics to sports.
They had a long conversation about their future plans.

senses.definition: your feelings or thoughts about a particular subject, rather than a fact

examples.x: His opinion on the matter was quite different from mine.
In his opinion, summer is the best season of the year.
Our teacher always encourages us to express our opinions in class.
They asked for her opinion on the new company policy.

senses.definition: a form of communication using spoken words

examples.x: After a long talk, they finally resolved their differences.
My parents had a talk with me about the importance of responsibility.
Their private talk made the rest of us curious.
They stayed up late into the night, lost in their talk about old times.
We had a serious talk about our future.

senses.definition: the act of talking to someone on the phone or an attempt to reach someone through a phone

examples.x: During the call, he shared the good news.
I got a call from the school about my son's performance.
I received a call from an unknown number.
Please hold the call, I will be back in a moment.
She makes a call to her family every Sunday.

senses.definition: a phone that we can carry with us and use anywhere because it has no wires

examples.x: Can you please mute your cell phone during the movie?
He dropped his cell phone and cracked the screen.
I forgot to bring my cell phone to the meeting.
My cell phone battery is running low.
She rarely uses her cell phone for making calls, mostly for texting.

senses.definition: a discussion, typically a serious one, between two or more people with different views

examples.x: His strong argument convinced the team to change their strategy.
She avoids getting into an argument with her boss.
The siblings had a silly argument about who got the bigger slice of cake.
Their constant arguments are causing problems in the relationship.
They had an argument about where to go for vacation.

senses.definition: an act or process of talking and sharing ideas in order to reach a decision or conclusion

examples.x: The book club meeting ended with a lively discussion about the novel.
The discussion on environmental policies was heated.
The family dinner turned into a discussion about their vacation plans.
The teacher encouraged a class discussion on the topic.
We had a lengthy discussion before reaching a decision.

senses.definition: an argument over something

examples.x: After the fight about the broken vase, they didn't talk for a week.
The fight about homework rules ended in a compromise.
The fight about money caused tension in their relationship.
Their constant fights about chores were driving their roommates crazy.
Their small fight about the TV remote turned into a huge argument.

senses.definition: a written or printed message that is sent to someone or an organization, company, etc.

examples.x: I received a thank-you letter from the charity I donated to.
He sent a letter of resignation to his boss.
He was delighted to get a letter of acceptance from the university.
I received a letter from my friend who lives abroad.

senses.definition: the system used for sending and delivering letters, packages, etc.

examples.x: The mail system in this country is very efficient.
There was a disruption in mail delivery due to the snowstorm.

senses.definition: a thin, paper cover in which we put and send a letter

examples.x: He wrote the address on the envelope.
I need to buy some envelopes for my Christmas cards.
She found an old envelope with photos in it.
She handed me an envelope with a note inside.
The envelope contained a surprise birthday card.

senses.definition: to use words and our voice to show what we are thinking or feeling

examples.x: He was saying that he wanted to quit his job and travel the world.
She is saying that she needs some space to think.
She said she loved the gift I gave her.
She says she enjoys reading books in her free time.
They said they were sorry for being late.

senses.definition: to use words and give someone information

examples.x: Did he tell you about the new project?
She told her family about her exciting job offer.
She told her friend about the new restaurant in town.
Why wasn't I told about the change in schedule?
Can you tell me about your vacation?

senses.definition: to talk about something with someone, often in a formal manner

examples.x: He wanted to discuss his concerns with the manager before making a formal complaint.
I discussed my health concerns with the doctor.
Let's discuss our plans for the weekend.
The manager discussed the sales targets with the staff.
Can we discuss this matter privately?

senses.definition: to go somewhere because we want to spend time with someone

examples.x: I love to visit my uncle because he tells great stories.
My brother visits me every time he's in town.
She's planning to visit her pen pal in France next year.
They visit their cousins during the holidays.
We should visit our old neighbors.

senses.definition: to answer someone by writing or saying something

examples.x: I need to reply to the email from my boss.
I'm waiting for them to reply to my proposal.
Please reply to the invitation as soon as possible.
He replied to the text message with a simple "Yes" to confirm the dinner reservation.
She replied to her friend's message with a heartfelt thank-you note for the birthday gift.

senses.definition: to speak to someone often angrily because one disagrees with them
to send a letter or package by post

examples.x: He mailed his application to the university yesterday.
I need to mail this package to my friend.
Please mail the invitations for the party to me.
She mails a letter to her grandmother every month.

senses.definition: with something or someone else

examples.x: My friends and I traveled together to Spain last summer.
They moved to a new city and started a life together.
She and her friend studied together for the final exam.
They traveled together across Europe last summer.

senses.definition: without stress or strong emotion

examples.x: Despite the chaos around her, she calmly continued her work.
Despite the fire alarm, everyone calmly evacuated the building.
Even in the face of danger, she remained calmly focused on the task.
He calmly explained his point of view.
I was shocked when he calmly accepted the criticism and promised to improve.

senses.definition: without anyone else

examples.x: He likes to eat lunch alone and enjoy some quiet time.
He spent the evening alone reading a book.
I am not brave enough to go camping alone.
I chose to stay alone at home during the holidays.
I traveled alone to Europe last summer.

senses.definition: relating to activities in which people meet each other for pleasure

examples.x: He's not very social and prefers to stay at home.
Our social calendar is full this month with various parties and gatherings.
She's the social organizer in our group and arranges all the meetups.
The club offers many social opportunities for its members.

senses.definition: to have a person, letter, or package physically delivered from one location to another, specifically by mail

examples.x: I need to send this important document to the head office by express mail.
Can you send the package to my home address instead of the office?
She decided to send a handwritten letter to her friend who lived overseas.
Please send the invoice to our accounting department for processing.
They promised to send the signed contract to us by the end of the week.

senses.definition: to be given something or to accept something that is sent

examples.x: Every morning, he receives a newspaper at his doorstep.
She was happy to receive a surprise gift on her birthday.
The charity received a large donation from an anonymous benefactor.
We received an invitation to their wedding.

senses.definition: to know something's meaning, particularly something that someone says

examples.x: Can you help me understand this equation?
She has a unique way of explaining things that makes it easy to understand complex topics.
He didn't understand the contract he was agreeing to.
The poor audio quality made the speaker difficult to understand.
After reading the explanation a few times, I finally understand the concept.

senses.definition: to fail to understand something or someone correctly

examples.x: He misunderstood the meeting time and arrived an hour early.
I think you misunderstand what I'm trying to say.
If you misunderstand the question, don't hesitate to ask for clarification.

senses.definition: to hold the same opinion as another person about something

examples.x: They agree that the movie was excellent.
We all agree that we need more resources for the project.
We both agree that this is the best restaurant in town.

senses.definition: to hold or give a different opinion about something

examples.x: They usually agree, but on this issue, they disagree.

senses.definition: to say yes to what is asked of you or offered to you

examples.x: He accepted the responsibility of caring for the dog.
He accepted the terms and conditions of the agreement.
I accepted the task, despite knowing it would be difficult.
My sister finally accepted the challenge to run a marathon.
They accepted the offer to stay at the beach house for the weekend.

senses.definition: to refuse to accept a proposal, idea, person, etc.

examples.x: He rejected the first draft of the report, asking for major revisions.
I had to reject the job offer due to the low salary.
She rejected the dress because it didn't fit her well.
The strict teacher rejected the student's late assignment.
They rejected our suggestion to change the design.

senses.definition: used to show our agreement or satisfaction with something

examples.x: All right, I will help you with your homework.
All right, I'll do the dishes tonight.
All right, I'll take care of the laundry.
All right, you can borrow my car for the day.
All right, you can play video games for an hour.

senses.definition: something that is expressed through things one says or writes

examples.x: His statement about the event was very detailed.
I disagreed with his statement about the movie.
She wrote a statement explaining her decision.
The company's mission statement emphasizes customer service.
The teacher asked for a statement from each student on the topic.

senses.definition: used to express a strong feeling of surprise, wonder, admiration, or amazement

examples.x: I can't believe you finished the race so quickly, wow!
Wow, I've never seen a building that tall.
Wow, look at that sunset; it's breathtaking.
Wow, you've really improved your basketball skills.
Wow, how did you manage to do all of that in one day?

senses.definition: used to show that we are angry, interested, etc.

examples.x: Ah, could you please turn down the music?
Ah, I've been looking for this book everywhere.
Ah, so you're the famous artist everyone's been talking about.
Ah, there's nothing like a cup of hot chocolate on a cold day.
I forgot to bring my umbrella, ah!

senses.definition: used to express surprise, realization, understanding

examples.x: Oh, I didn't know you could play the guitar.
Oh, I didn't realize the time.
Oh, I didn't realize you were a vegetarian.
Oh, I wasn't aware that the meeting was rescheduled.
Oh, I get it now, thanks for explaining.

senses.definition: used as another way of saying 'yes'

examples.x: Yeah, I agree with you on that point.
Yeah, I know how to fix this problem.
Yeah, I'll be able to come to your party.
Yeah, I'm going to the concert this weekend.
Yeah, I've finished the report for the meeting.

senses.definition: used to say hi

examples.x: Hey, how's it going?
Hey, have you heard the latest news?
Hey, it's great to see you again!
Hey, what's up?
Hey!
Hey, welcome to the party!

senses.definition: a brief informal written message

examples.x: John passed a funny note to his classmate during the boring lecture.
Mark left a note on the fridge to remind his roommate to buy milk.
Sarah sent a sweet note to her friend to wish her good luck on the exam.
The boss left a note of appreciation on the employee's desk for a job well done.

senses.definition: a written or spoken request to someone, asking them to attend a party or event

examples.x: She received an invitation to her friend's birthday party next weekend.
The couple sent out elegant wedding invitations to all their family and friends.
He was thrilled to receive an invitation to interview for his dream job.
They extended a warm invitation to their neighbors to join them for a backyard barbecue.
The invitation included the date, time, and venue of the event.

senses.definition: to exchange information, news, ideas, etc. with someone

examples.x: She communicates effectively with her team members.
Right now, he is communicating with clients over a video call.
He is communicating with colleagues through instant messaging.
They communicate their plans through weekly meetings.
The manager effectively communicated the new policy to the entire staff.

senses.definition: to express your annoyance, unhappiness, or dissatisfaction about something

examples.x: Emily likes to complain about the long commute to work every morning.
The customers decided to complain about the poor service they received at the restaurant.
Instead of complaining, try to find constructive solutions to the challenges you face.
Tom didn't hesitate to complain when his order at the restaurant arrived with the wrong items.
Rather than complaining about the weather, Sarah decided to make the best of the rainy day and stayed indoors reading a book.

senses.definition: to make a formal or friendly request to someone to come somewhere or join something

examples.x: She invites friends over for dinner every Friday night.
The bride and groom are inviting friends and family to their wedding.
We decided to invite the neighbors to our annual barbecue.
She invited me to dinner at her favorite restaurant.

senses.definition: to say the sound of a letter or word correctly or in a specific way

examples.x: She pronounces each word with clarity during the language class.
He pronounced the foreign name perfectly after practicing for hours.
The teacher emphasized the importance of pronouncing vowels accurately.
Before the training, she had struggled to pronounce certain words, but now she is much more confident.
She learned to pronounce difficult words with ease.
